smith-v-abc-corp-2022.name: Smith v. ABC Corp
smith-v-abc-corp-2022.holding: Time is of the essence clauses must be explicitly stated and cannot be implied from the circumstances alone.
smith-v-abc-corp-2022.summary: The court held that where a commercial contract does not expressly state that time is of the essence, a reasonable-time standard governs delivery obligations.
smith-v-abc-corp-2022.disposition: Judgment for plaintiff affirmed in part.

johnson-v-xyz-industries-2020.name: Johnson v. XYZ Industries
johnson-v-xyz-industries-2020.holding: Commercial contracts are presumed to include reasonable delivery terms informed by industry standards.
johnson-v-xyz-industries-2020.summary: The California Supreme Court clarified that industry standards are relevant, though not dispositive, when determining a reasonable time for performance.

davis-v-tech-solutions-2021.name: Davis v. Tech Solutions
davis-v-tech-solutions-2021.holding: A material breach requires substantial non-performance; minor delays are generally not material.
davis-v-tech-solutions-2021.summary: The court defined the threshold for material breach in commercial delivery disputes and addressed the calculation of foreseeable damages.

hadley-v-baxendale-1854.name: Hadley v. Baxendale
hadley-v-baxendale-1854.holding: Consequential damages are recoverable only where they were reasonably foreseeable at the time of contracting.
hadley-v-baxendale-1854.summary: The foundational rule on the foreseeability of contract damages, still routinely cited in United States commercial litigation.
